page: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz with 32 physical cores and a single thread per core configuration. The CPU resources are allocated on a shared basis. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a 500 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU is present.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong processing capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3144.37 places it in the top tier (top 10%), while the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 100519.25 is rated as strong (top 25%). The AMD Genoa processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate vector calculations and machine learning inference workloads. Cryptographic operations are aided by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instructions.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective alternative for compute-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated, non-disruptive CPU pinning. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for parallel processing tasks, software compilation, batch processing, and web serving. It is also well-suited for workloads utilizing vector extensions, such as cryptography and data transformation pipelines, where high core density and fast NVMe storage are prioritized over massive memory footprints.
description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 32 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this shared-CPU instance includes 32 physical cores and a 500 GB NVMe SSD. In performance benchmarks, the server achieves top-tier results, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3144.37 (top 10%) and a strong multi-core score of 100519.25 (top 25%). The processor supports advanced vector and cryptographic instructions, including AVX-512 and AES. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server provides a cost-effective profile for parallel processing, software compilation, and web serving workloads that benefit from high core density and fast local storage without requiring dedicated CPU allocation.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-500s-amd features 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 500 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier single-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-density, shared-CPU instance featuring AMD Genoa processors and NVMe storage for parallel processing and compute-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs with shared CPU allocation
64.0 GB system memory and 500 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core stress-ng CPU performance
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AES
Optimized for parallel processing, software compilation, and web serving
